bill_or_statute: 2022 AL HB272 / Act 2022-133 / Ala. Code sec. 13A-11-85
coding_notes: HB272 removed the concealed pistol permit requirement for otherwise eligible adults. Open carry was already broadly available with location limits.

bill_or_statute: 2003 AK HB102 / ch. 62 SLA 2003 / Alaska Stat. sec. 11.61.220
coding_notes: Alaska DPS confirms permitless concealed carry for adults 21 and older. Optional permits remain for reciprocity and federal school-zone purposes.

bill_or_statute: 2010 AZ SB1108 / Ariz. Rev. Stat. sec. 13-3102
coding_notes: SB1108 removed the permit requirement for adults 21 and older to carry discreetly. Permits remain relevant for alcohol-serving establishments and reciprocity.

bill_or_statute: 2023 AR SB480 / Act 777 / Ark. Code sec. 5-73-329
coding_notes: Arkansas is currently listed by Giffords as permitless effective approximately 2021-08-29 and Act 777 later clarified that a concealed handgun license is not required. Because the clean annual treatment date depends on whether the analysis treats the 2021 or 2023 clarification as adoption, Arkansas is reviewed but not added to the within-panel treatment map.

bill_or_statute: No statewide permitless concealed carry law through 2024 / Cal. Penal Code secs. 26150 and 26155
coding_notes: California remains a concealed-carry permit state and generally does not allow permitless open handgun carry. No within-panel permitless concealed-carry adoption is coded.

bill_or_statute: No statewide permitless concealed carry law through 2024 / Colo. Rev. Stat. sec. 18-12-203
coding_notes: Colorado remains a concealed-handgun permit state. Open carry is generally allowed but local restrictions can apply so the state is not treated as permitless concealed carry.

bill_or_statute: No statewide permitless concealed carry law through 2024 / Conn. Gen. Stat. sec. 29-28
coding_notes: Connecticut remains a permit-required carry state and generally prohibits permitless open carry after the 2023 open-carry restriction.

bill_or_statute: No statewide permitless concealed carry law through 2024 / Del. Code tit. 11 sec. 1441
coding_notes: Delaware requires a concealed deadly weapon license for concealed carry. Permitless open carry does not make the state a permitless concealed-carry adopter.
Florida HB543 removed the concealed-carry license requirement. Open carry remained generally prohibited.

bill_or_statute: 2022 GA SB319 / Georgia Constitutional Carry Act / Ga. Code Ann. sec. 16-11-125.1
coding_notes: SB319 made lawful weapons carriers eligible to carry without a weapons carry license. The governor signed it on April 12 and the state described the effective date as April 12.

bill_or_statute: No statewide permitless concealed carry law through 2024 / Haw. Rev. Stat. sec. 134-9
coding_notes: Hawaii remains a license-required public-carry state. No statewide permitless concealed-carry adoption is coded.

bill_or_statute: 2016 ID S1389 / Idaho Code sec. 18-3302
coding_notes: The 2016 law created permitless concealed carry for Idaho residents 21 and older. Later 2019 and 2020 changes expanded age and nonresident scope.

bill_or_statute: No statewide permitless concealed carry law through 2024 / 430 ILCS 66
coding_notes: Illinois remains a concealed-carry license state and generally prohibits permitless open carry.

bill_or_statute: 2022 IN HB1296 / HEA 1296 / Ind. Code sec. 35-47-2-3
coding_notes: Indiana State Police guidance states that the July 1 2022 law ended the permit requirement to legally carry conceal or transport a handgun for proper persons.

bill_or_statute: 2021 IA HF756 / Iowa Code sec. 724.5
coding_notes: HF756 made carry permits optional and also changed handgun purchase-permit rules. Age coding should be rechecked against Iowa Code before manuscript use.

bill_or_statute: 2015 KS SB45 / Kan. Stat. Ann. sec. 21-6302
coding_notes: Kansas bill history records approval by the governor and a July 1 2015 effective date.

bill_or_statute: 2019 KY SB150 / Ky. Rev. Stat. secs. 237.109 and 527.020
coding_notes: Kentucky State Police reports permitless concealed carry as of June 28 2019 for adults 21 and older who may lawfully possess firearms.

bill_or_statute: 2024 LA SB1 / Act 1 / La. Rev. Stat. sec. 14:95
coding_notes: SB1 became Act 1 on March 5 2024 with an effective date of July 4 2024. The annual panel codes the first affected year as 2024.

bill_or_statute: 2015 ME LD652 / Public Law 2015 ch. 327 / Me. Rev. Stat. tit. 25 sec. 2001-A
coding_notes: Maine State Police identifies LD652 and the October 15 2015 effective date for permitless concealed handgun carry.

bill_or_statute: No statewide permitless concealed carry law through 2024 / Md. Public Safety sec. 5-306
coding_notes: Maryland requires a wear-and-carry permit for public handgun carry. No permitless concealed-carry treatment year is coded.

bill_or_statute: No statewide permitless concealed carry law through 2024 / Mass. Gen. Laws ch. 140 sec. 131
coding_notes: Massachusetts remains a license-required carry state. No permitless concealed-carry adoption is coded.

bill_or_statute: No statewide permitless concealed carry law through 2024 / Mich. Comp. Laws sec. 28.425c
coding_notes: Michigan requires a concealed pistol license for concealed carry. Permitless open carry is treated separately and is not a concealed-carry adoption.

bill_or_statute: No statewide permitless concealed carry law through 2024 / Minn. Stat. sec. 624.714
coding_notes: Minnesota requires a permit to carry pistols in public. No permitless concealed-carry treatment year is coded.

bill_or_statute: 2015 MS SB2394 / Miss. Code secs. 97-37-1 and 45-9-101 with 2016 HB786 expansion
coding_notes: Current panel year 2015 matches the purse bag briefcase or enclosed-case expansion. Giffords cites 2016 HB786 for the broader holster expansion so this row remains partial.

bill_or_statute: 2016 MO SB656 / Mo. Rev. Stat. sec. 571.030
coding_notes: Missouri veto override occurred in September 2016 and the concealed-carry provision had a January 1 2017 delayed effective date. Local open-carry restrictions can apply to people without permits.

bill_or_statute: 2021 MT HB102 / Mont. Code Ann. sec. 45-8-316
coding_notes: HB102 expanded statewide permitless concealed carry beyond prior rural and outdoor-activity exceptions.

bill_or_statute: 2023 NE LB77 / Neb. Rev. Stat. sec. 28-1202
coding_notes: Nebraska Legislature records LB77 approval by the governor on April 25 2023. Nebraska State Patrol states LB77 took full legal effect on September 2 2023.

bill_or_statute: No statewide permitless concealed carry law through 2024 / Nev. Rev. Stat. sec. 202.3653
coding_notes: Nevada requires a permit for concealed firearm carry. Open carry is generally allowed without a permit and is documented separately.
SB12 repealed the licensing requirement for carrying a concealed pistol or revolver and was effective immediately.

bill_or_statute: No statewide permitless concealed carry law through 2024 / N.J. Stat. sec. 2C:58-4
coding_notes: New Jersey requires a permit to carry a handgun and does not provide statewide permitless public carry.

bill_or_statute: No statewide permitless concealed carry law through 2024 / N.M. Stat. sec. 29-19-4
coding_notes: New Mexico requires a concealed handgun license for concealed carry. Permitless open carry is not coded as permitless concealed carry.

bill_or_statute: No statewide permitless concealed carry law through 2024 / N.Y. Penal Law sec. 400.00
coding_notes: New York remains a license-required handgun carry state and generally does not allow permitless open handgun carry.

bill_or_statute: No statewide permitless concealed carry law through 2024 / N.C. Gen. Stat. sec. 14-415.11
coding_notes: North Carolina requires a concealed handgun permit for concealed carry. Open carry is generally allowed with local limits and is not coded as concealed permitless carry.
North Dakota 2017 law applied to residents within North Dakota. Later law expanded nonresident scope in 2023. Open carry remained restricted or partial.

bill_or_statute: 2022 OH SB215 / Ohio Rev. Code sec. 2923.111
coding_notes: Ohio Legislative Service Commission final analysis identifies June 13 2022 as the effective date and defines qualifying adults as 21 and older.

bill_or_statute: 2019 OK HB2597 / Okla. Stat. tit. 21 secs. 1277 and 1290.1-1290.26
coding_notes: Oklahoma summary materials describe HB2597 as effective November 1 2019 and allowing concealed or unconcealed carry without a license for eligible adults.

bill_or_statute: No statewide permitless concealed carry law through 2024 / Or. Rev. Stat. sec. 166.291
coding_notes: Oregon requires a concealed handgun license for concealed carry. Open carry can be lawful with local loaded-carry restrictions and is not coded as permitless concealed carry.

bill_or_statute: No statewide permitless concealed carry law through 2024 / 18 Pa. Cons. Stat. secs. 6106 and 6109
coding_notes: Pennsylvania requires a license for concealed carry and for vehicle carry. Open carry is generally allowed outside Philadelphia but is separate from concealed permitless carry.

bill_or_statute: No statewide permitless concealed carry law through 2024 / R.I. Gen. Laws secs. 11-47-11 and 11-47-18
coding_notes: Rhode Island requires a license or permit for public handgun carry. No permitless concealed-carry adoption is coded.

bill_or_statute: 2024 SC H3594 / Constitutional Carry Guidance
coding_notes: SLED guidance states H.3594 was signed by the governor on March 7 2024 and was effective immediately for open or concealed carry without a permit.

bill_or_statute: 2019 SD SB47 / S.D. Codified Laws secs. 23-7-7 to 23-7-8.6
coding_notes: South Dakota bill history records the governor signing SB47 on January 31 2019. The concealed-carry change took effect July 1 2019.

bill_or_statute: 2021 TN SB765 / HB786 / Tenn. Code Ann. sec. 39-17-1307
coding_notes: Tennessee law permitted open or concealed handgun carry without a permit for eligible adults as of July 1 2021. The general age rule changed after later litigation.

bill_or_statute: 2021 TX HB1927 / Firearm Carry Act of 2021 / Tex. Penal Code sec. 46.02
coding_notes: Texas Legislative Reference Library records HB1927 as effective September 1 2021. Texas State Law Library summarizes the license-to-carry change.

bill_or_statute: 2021 UT HB60 / Utah Code Ann. sec. 53-5a-102.2
coding_notes: Utah BCI identifies HB60 and the May 5 2021 effective date. Permitless open-carry scope should be checked against Utah Code before using the field analytically.

bill_or_statute: No permit-to-carry requirement before panel start / Vermont carry
coding_notes: Vermont does not issue concealed-carry permits or require a permit to carry concealed. This is a baseline permitless state rather than a within-panel adoption event.

bill_or_statute: No statewide permitless concealed carry law through 2024 / Va. Code sec. 18.2-308
coding_notes: Virginia requires a concealed handgun permit for concealed carry. Open carry is generally allowed with location and local restrictions and is not coded as concealed permitless carry.

bill_or_statute: No statewide permitless concealed carry law through 2024 / Wash. Rev. Code secs. 9.41.050 and 9.41.070
coding_notes: Washington requires a concealed pistol license for concealed pistol carry and for loaded pistol carry in a vehicle. Open carry is not coded as permitless concealed carry.

bill_or_statute: 2016 WV HB4145 / W. Va. Code sec. 61-7-3
coding_notes: The legislature overrode the governor veto on March 5 2016 and contemporary state press reported the law effective May 24 2016.

bill_or_statute: No statewide permitless concealed carry law through 2024 / Wis. Stat. secs. 175.60 and 941.23
coding_notes: Wisconsin requires a concealed carry weapon license for concealed carry. Open carry is generally allowed but loaded handgun carry in a vehicle can require licensure.

bill_or_statute: 2011 WY SF0047 / Wyo. Stat. Ann. sec. 6-8-104
coding_notes: Wyoming 2011 law applied to residents at adoption and was expanded to nonresidents in 2021. Open carry was already lawful without a permit.
